title: fraxinus willdenowiana
intro: Fraxinus willdenowiana, also known as the Australian ash or willow ash, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The bark is rich in salicylic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, providing relief from pain and swelling. Fraxinus willdenowiana can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
